How Crypto Cycles Work

Accumulation
Low sentiment, smart money building
Expansion
Recovery, mainstream coverage begins
Peak / Euphoria
Everyone bullish, retail FOMO
Contraction
Drawdown, capitulation

▶ Current position: Bear/Accumulation transition  |  April 2024 halving → historically 12–18 months to next bull phase

21 / 49

Calibrate Your Strategy
to Your Profile

6 DIMENSIONS (SCORE 1–10)
  • Risk Tolerance — How much drawdown can you stomach?
  • Time Horizon — 1 year? 5? 10+?
  • Knowledge Level — Beginner to advanced
  • Active vs Passive — How hands-on?
  • Income Needs — Growth or yield?
  • Tax Situation — Structure matters

SCORE YOURSELF NOW

Your strategy should match your profile.

22 / 49

Risk in Crypto: A Complete Framework

📈
Market Risk

Position sizing, staged entry, time horizon

🔒
Security Risk

Hardware wallet, seed phrase, exchange hygiene

💧
Liquidity Risk

BTC/ETH core, avoid illiquid alts for large positions

🧠
Emotional Risk

Written framework, pre-set rules, limit daily checking

Only allocate what you are comfortable watching drop 50% without changing your strategy.
23 / 49

Pre-Set Your Rules.
In a Calm Moment.

✅ BUY MORE When

  • Price hits pre-defined accumulation targets
  • Macro improves (rate cuts, conflict resolution)
  • On-chain reaches historical accumulation zones

✋ HOLD When

Everything else. Including “price is going up” and “price is going down.”

If it is not in your framework, the answer is hold.

🔴 REDUCE When

  • Pre-defined price targets hit
  • Allocation exceeds pre-set max %
  • Fundamental thesis changes materially

26 / 49

Start Simple. Add Complexity
Only When Earned.

Variable: Stablecoins
0–20% Selective Alts
20–30% Ethereum
50–70% Bitcoin (Foundation)
  • Adding 1–5% Bitcoin improves portfolio risk-adjusted returns (research-backed)
  • Most members start at 5–15% of total investable assets
  • Only add alts where you deeply know the thesis
Start with Bitcoin. Get that right first.

Dry Powder

Yield

Risk Management

32 / 49

DeFi: Start Small,
Learn Deliberately

OPPORTUNITIES
  • Stablecoin lending protocols — earn yield
  • RWA tokenisation: NASDAQ, ICE, S&P indices moving on-chain
  • Tokenised treasuries and bonds
RISKS
  • Smart contract bugs — use battle-tested protocols only
  • Complexity — if you don’t understand it, don’t put large capital in
  • Elevated yield = elevated risk — always ask why
This is a Phase 2 module for a reason. Get foundations right first.
33 / 49

Bitcoin Lending &
Passive Income

Deposit BTC → lends to overcollateralised borrowers → you earn yield

CentralisedDecentralised
CustodyPlatform holdsSmart contract
ComplexitySimpleMore complex
RiskPlatform failureSmart contract bugs
KEY RISKS
  • • Platform risk — companies can fail (FTX)
  • • Lock-up periods — cannot react to price moves
  • • Elevated yield = elevated risk
TAX NOTE (AU)

Interest = ordinary income. Track every payment date + value.

Sell Strategy
07
Tax & Preservation

The step that separates wealth builders from wealth watchers.

Ask yourself: do you have a written sell strategy right now? Most don’t. That is the most expensive gap in any portfolio.

35 / 49

The Pattern That Destroys
Portfolios — Repeatedly

The sell decision must be made in a calm moment. Not a panicking one. Not an euphoric one.
36 / 49

Three Components.
One Written Document.

Target-Based Selling

Define price targets → sell defined % at each

Example: 10% at 2×, 20% at 3×, 30% at 5×

Time-Based Selling

Take some profits at defined cycle points regardless of price

You do not have to call the top. Just sell some before it.

Indicator-Based Selling

CS research flags on-chain signals: NUPL, MVRV, LTH distribution

When signals fire, have your plan ready.

A sell strategy is not about leaving money on the table. It is about not giving it all back.
37 / 49

The Profit-Taking Pyramid

25–35% Never Sell
25–30% Cycle Peak Signals
20% at 5× Entry
15% at 3× Entry
10% at 2× Entry
LevelTriggerAction
2× entryFirst takeSell 10%
3× entrySecond takeSell 15%
5× entryThird takeSell 20%
Cycle peakMajor reductionSell 25–30%
Long-termNever sellHold 25–35%
The people who won the last cycle took profits in stages.

01

02

04

06
Sell Strategy
07
Tax & Preservation
  • Build legal/financial structures that protect your wealth after you have built it
  • CGT events: every trade, swap, DeFi interaction is potentially taxable
  • This is not financial advice — it is the framework for getting the right advice
40 / 49

Every Disposal Is a
CGT Event. Every One.

DISPOSALS INCLUDE
  • Selling for AUD
  • Crypto-to-crypto swaps
  • DeFi transactions
  • Spending crypto
12+ month hold = 50% CGT discount
Time horizon affects your tax bill as much as your price target
RECORD-KEEPING RULES
  • Use Koinly or CryptoTaxCalculator — start now, not at tax time
  • Every transaction: date, value at time, fees
  • Every DeFi interaction: all of it needs to be recorded
The ATO has significantly increased its focus on crypto.
41 / 49

Tax Structures for
High-Net-Worth Investors

🏢
SMSF
  • • Hold Bitcoin inside super
  • • 15% tax rate vs. marginal rate
  • • Pension phase: potentially 0% on gains
  • • Requires specialist SMSF advisor
👪
Family Trust
  • • Distribute gains to lower-tax beneficiaries
  • • Estate planning benefits
  • • Know this exists before you sell
🏛
Company Structure
  • • 25–30% corporate tax rate
  • • Less flexibility, right for some

This is not financial advice. Speak to a specialist.

42 / 49

Building Wealth and
Keeping It Are Different Skills

  • Estate planning must include crypto — who has the seed phrase? How is it transferred?
  • Build an access document: sealed letter to family/executor with instructions
  • Insurance: check if crypto is covered under existing policies. Most are not.
4 QUESTIONS FOR ANY ADVISOR
  • 1. Experience with crypto CGT and self-custody in Australia?
  • 2. Understand DeFi taxation?
  • 3. Helped clients set up SMSF for crypto?
  • 4. Can provide a client reference?

CS connects members with crypto-specialist advisors — ask your CSM.

45 / 49

The Four Questions
That Matter Most

1
What is your target allocation?

% of total investable assets (excl. primary residence)

2
What is your time horizon?

Minimum hold period you are genuinely committed to

3
What is your risk tolerance?

A 40–50% drawdown is normal. How did you feel the last 3 months?

4
What does success look like?

At what price, in what conditions, in what size do you take profits? Written. In advance. Calm.
